payload.answer: The proposed 500 one-row memory cards are too granular for durable memory as-is. Offer repair choices that preserve the table’s useful context without committing each row as a separate memory.
repair_options: Do not save the 500 row-level cards; instead create a single summarized memory capturing the CSV/table topic, purpose, source, and where the full data can be found.
Do not save the 500 row-level cards; instead save only high-level patterns, conclusions, or user-relevant preferences derived from the table.
Ask the user which specific rows, columns, or insights are important enough to remember before saving anything.
If exact row data must be retained, store it as an external file or document reference rather than atomizing it into memory cards.
If some rows represent distinct durable facts, save only those selected facts as separate memories and discard the rest.
Keep both: a compact summary memory plus an external reference to the full CSV, without converting every row into an individual memory card.
